response.justification: Strong foundational engineer with 20 years of experience and recent cloud/AI infrastructure work, but falls short of the strategic AI expertise the role demands. The candidate excels in hands-on engineering and technical breadth across multiple domains, yet has only 2 months of demonstrated AI/agent work and lacks deep, proven expertise in building AI products or platforms. Lacks the advanced degree and recognized thought leadership in AI that the preferred qualifications emphasize. Better positioned for Principal Engineer in Cloud Infrastructure than AI Strategy.
strengths: Exceeds base requirements with 20 years of continuous engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
Recent hands-on AI/agent infrastructure work at re:cinq with MCP and developer tooling frameworks shows current technical direction and builder fluency
Demonstrated leadership and knowledge-sharing through mentoring (Refactoring Dojos), public talks (FOSS4G), workshops, and successful open-source projects (GISCollective)
concerns: Only 2 months in current AI-focused role—lacks proven track record of building recognized AI products or platforms required for the AI Strategy focus
No advanced degree in AI/ML (Master's/PhD preferred) and no evidence of deep machine learning or AI model expertise despite 20-year career

Career trajectory emphasizes hands-on execution and full-stack development rather than strategic advisory; limited evidence of influencing product vision or advising customers on cutting-edge AI adoption
